solution.p: To subtract two fractions with different denominators, we first have to find a common denominator.
In this case, the least common multiple of 40 and 8 is 40.
The first fraction already has the common denominator.
Look at the second fraction \displaystyle{\frac{5}{8}}. To change the denominator to 40, we multiply 5 at both the top and bottom, and we have:
Finally, we can do the subtraction. The whole process is:
